2604.15124.title: Blinded Multi-Rater Comparative Evaluation of a Large Language Model and Clinician-Authored Responses in CGM-Informed Diabetes Counseling
2604.15124.paper_summary: Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) is central to diabetes care, but explaining CGM patterns clearly and empathetically remains time-intensive. Evidence for retrieval-grounded large language model (LLM) systems in CGM-informed counseling remains limited. To evaluate whether a retrieval-grounded LLM-based conversational agent (CA) could support patient understanding of CGM data and preparation for routine diabetes consultations. We developed a retrieval-grounded LLM-based CA for CGM interpretation and diabetes counseling support. The system generated plain-language responses while avoiding individualized therapeutic advice. Twelve CGM-informed cases were constructed from publicly available datasets. Between Oct 2025 and Feb 2026, 6 senior UK diabetes clinicians each reviewed 2 assigned cases and answered 24 questions. In a blinded multi-rater evaluation, each CA-generated and clinician-authored response was independently rated by 3 clinicians on 6 quality dimensions. Safety flags and perceived source labels were also recorded. Primary analyses used linear mixed-effects models. A total of 288 unique responses (144 CA and 144 clinician) generated 864 ratings. The CA received higher quality scores than clinician responses (mean 4.37 vs 3.58), with an estimated mean difference of 0.782 points (95% CI 0.692-0.872; P<.001). The largest differences were for empathy (1.062, 95% CI 0.948-1.177) and actionability (0.992, 95% CI 0.877-1.106). Safety flag distributions were similar, with major concerns rare in both groups (3/432, 0.7% each). Retrieval-grounded LLM systems may have value as adjunct tools for CGM review, patient education, and preconsultation preparation. However, these findings do not support autonomous therapeutic decision-making or unsupervised real-world use.
2604.15124.paper_summary_zh: 持續血糖監測（CGM）在糖尿病護理中至關重要，但清晰且具同理心地解釋CGM模式仍然需要耗費大量時間。基於檢索的語言模型（LLM）系統在CGM輔導中的證據仍然有限。本研究旨在評估基於檢索的LLM對話代理（CA）是否能支持患者理解CGM數據並為常規糖尿病諮詢做準備。我們開發了一個基於檢索的LLM的CA，用於CGM解釋和糖尿病輔導支持。該系統生成了通俗易懂的回應，同時避免提供個性化的治療建議。從公開可用的數據集中構建了十二個CGM相關案例。在2025年10月至2026年2月期間，6位英國糖尿病臨床醫生各自審查了2個指定案例並回答了24個問題。在盲法多評估者評估中，每個CA生成的回應和臨床醫生撰寫的回應均由3位臨床醫生在6個質量維度上獨立評分。安全標記和感知來源標籤也被記錄下來。主要分析使用線性混合效應模型。總共生成了288個獨特回應（144個CA和144個臨床醫生），產生了864個評分。CA的質量分數高於臨床醫生的回應（平均4.37對3.58），估計平均差異為0.782分（95% CI 0.692-0.872；P<.001）。最大的差異出現在同理心（1.062，95% CI 0.948-1.177）和可行性（0.992，95% CI 0.877-1.106）。安全標記的分佈相似，兩組中主要關注事項均較少（3/432，0.7%各）。基於檢索的LLM系統可能作為CGM審查、患者教育和預備諮詢的輔助工具具有價值。然而，這些發現並不支持自主的治療決策或無監督的現實世界使用。
2604.15124.author: Zhijun Guo et.al.
2604.15124.authors: Zhijun Guo, Alvina Lai, Emmanouil Korakas, Aristeidis Vagenas, Irshad Ahamed, Christo Albor, Hengrui Zhang, Justin Healy, Kezhi Li
